claim: AI is extremely sycophantic: it agrees with bad ideas, says you're great, and validates untrue claims; this is especially dangerous for children with still-forming minds
actionable: When using AI with children, teach them that AI will agree even when wrong; verify important claims with external sources before trusting AI advice

claim: AI hallucinations persist despite improvements; AI confidently states false information; there is no way to fully prevent hallucinations (though frequency is reducing)
actionable: Always verify AI outputs independently; treat AI hallucinations as a persistent problem even with latest models, especially for factual claims

claim: Character AI saw children develop deep emotional relationships with AI through role-play, mistaking it for a real person; this led to Character AI restricting teen access and lawsuits
actionable: Supervise children's AI interactions; explicitly teach that AI is not a real person and developing emotional attachments is not healthy

claim: AI environmental impact (0.3-3g CO2 per query) is far lower than cars (170g CO2/km), flying (90-150g CO2/km), or clothing manufacturing (2,000-7,000g per T-shirt)
actionable: Counter false claims about AI's environmental impact; provide data showing AI's footprint is smaller than many everyday activities

claim: Modern AI data centers use closed-loop water cooling systems (no external water source) rather than evaporative cooling; Google, Meta, AWS, and Microsoft rolling out liquid-cooled facilities in 2025
actionable: Reference closed-loop cooling data when addressing concerns about AI water consumption; modern data centers have minimal water footprint
